title: Automatically preload and refresh the authoritative drug database (500+ entries)

requirements.text: Implement automatic population of the backend authoritative drug database so that on first run (and after upgrade, if the store is empty) the canister contains 500+ drug entries without requiring a manual import.
acceptanceCriteria: Calling the existing frontend query hooks (useGetAllDrugs/useGetApprovedDrugs/useGetBannedDrugs) returns non-empty results on a fresh deployment without any manual admin action.
On a fresh deployment, actor.getAllDrugs() returns at least 500 drugs.
The auto-population logic does not overwrite existing drug keys if the database is already populated.

requirements.text: Ensure the expanded canonical drug dataset (550+ entries across categories) is what populates the authoritative drug list shown in the Drug Database UI, matching the existing project goal of an expanded dataset.
acceptanceCriteria: The dataset used to seed the backend contains more than 550 total approved+banned entries spanning multiple categories (e.g., antibiotics, painkillers, FDCs, vitamins, other).
The Drug Database UI counts (total/approved/banned) match the backend authoritative dataset after seeding and after Refresh & Verify.

requirements.text: Make the existing “Refresh & Verify” workflow reliable by ensuring backend methods required by the frontend hooks exist and return consistent data: getAllDrugs, getApprovedDrugs, getBannedDrugs, getCategorizedDrugs, refreshAndVerifyDrugTable, getLastDrugVerification, getDrugTableVerificationReport, and getDrugTableLastRefreshTimestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: The Drug Database UI loads without runtime errors related to missing canister methods.
Clicking “Refresh & Verify” in the Drug Database UI completes successfully and updates the ‘Last backend refresh’ timestamp.
After Refresh & Verify completes, React Query invalidations result in updated lists being rendered (no stale counts).

requirements.text: Add/adjust the Drug Database UI header text to match the project requirement: display the header text “Complete CDSCO Drug Database | 500+ Drugs | Auto-Updated” prominently in the drug table module.

quotes: Responsive professional UI for academic use with filters (Status, Year, Category), drug/ingredient search, sortable columns, and header text "Complete CDSCO Drug Database | 500+ Drugs | Auto-Updated"

acceptanceCriteria: The specified header text is visible at the top of the Drug Database module on both desktop and mobile widths.

requirements.text: Apply a coherent, professional academic visual theme across the Drug Database module (cards, table header, badges, buttons, and banners) using a consistent neutral palette (avoid introducing blue/purple as primary brand colors unless already required) and consistent typography hierarchy.
acceptanceCriteria: Drug Database module styling appears consistent across tabs (All/Approved/Banned) and in dark/light themes.
No new primary brand reliance on blue/purple gradients is introduced as the dominant style in this module (existing elements may be adjusted to match the new theme).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; do not introduce additional backend services.
Only create/modify backend/migration.mo if needed for upgrading existing stable state; follow the existing conditional migration policy.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or any frontend immutablePaths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT; compose components instead.
Use English for all user-facing text.
nonGoals: Integrating third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
Deploying to external cloud providers or using external databases.

Guaranteeing live scraping/parsing of CDSCO PDFs or websites beyond the scope of a deterministic built-in seed + manual/admin import (unless explicitly requested later with exact URLs and parsing rules).

goalsToAdd: Automatically seed the authoritative drug database with 500+ (ideally 550+) entries so the app works immediately without manual import.
preferencesToAdd: Use English for user-facing UI text.
